(P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市八条町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:菅田神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134888232}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 菅田神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 21 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 菅田比古命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月15日 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = 一夜松天神・八条天神社 |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''菅田神社'''（すがたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式 |access-date=2024-4-23}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。全国、{{jalink|鍛冶|Q838566}}の神の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}である。 古くは、現社地の南約300メートルにあり、{{jalink|大永|Q1193564}}3(1523)年ごろ、鎮座したと言われている{{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。 === 由緒 === 昔、美しい女神が{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂の「菅田池」から現れ、大和郡山市八条町の神楽田の所でしばらく休んでから、そこから西の方へ行って鎮座しました。すると、そこが一夜にして鬱蒼(うっそう)とした松林になったので、誰云うとなく、その土地を「一夜松」と呼び、今「一夜松天神」「八条天神社」とも呼ばれる現在の菅田神社が鎮座する社頭掲示板より。『佐保川其西方を流れ、菅田池畔にのぞむ、堀河百首平安時代後期の歌集で、長治2年(1105)ごろの成立か。に「をとめ子がすがたの池のはちす葉は心よげにも花さきにけり」と、以って景勝の地たるを知るべし』と、{{jalink|明治神社誌料}}に記されている。 === 概要 === 祭神は、菅田比古命。菅田比古命の祖で鍛冶の神とされる{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750}}の説{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=神祇資料 |access-date=2024-4-24}}もあるが、{{jalink|平群郡|Q11483501}}({{jalink|明治時代}}の八条村は平群郡及び、{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}の管轄で、昭和28年に{{jalink|郡山町 (奈良県)||郡山町}}に編入される)の{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}には、菅田比古命が記されている{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=平群郡 神社明細帳 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。菅田比古命は、菅田氏の祖で『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「菅田首{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}之後也」と記されている。 なお、近世以前は、八条天神神社・一夜松天神とも呼ばれ、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた。一夜にして松が生えたという奇跡が言い伝えられており、一夜松の幹の表皮部分が{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}に{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}されている{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。{{jalink|元和 (日本)|Q635245|元和}}5年(1619年)の{{jalink|石燈籠}}には、『一夜松金琳宮』と刻され、一夜松金琳寺という{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}があったことが推定される。所在は、{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂北菅田町の春日神社元は、「天久斯比止都命を祀ったものであろう」(天理市史より)の西方天理市史 上巻。 {{jalink|本殿|Q2044994}}は、{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}で{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}との間に{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}が設けられている。本殿前には、『天満宮御神前』と刻された石燈籠もあり、{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}の神社であったことがうかがえる。 また、同じく添下郡の式内社で大和郡山市筒井町に鎮座する、{{jalink|菅田比売神社|Q134893097}}(祭神は菅田比売命・天久斯麻比止都命2座特選神名牒及び、国立国会図書館サーチ「式内社の研究 第2巻」による。)ただし、添下郡神社明細帳では、伊豆能賣神1座。と、対をなしていると考えられている。 なお、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|小野市|Q911717}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (小野市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)と、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|近江八幡市|Q385461}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (近江八幡市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)の祭神は、天目一箇神である。 === 境内社 === *厳島神社 (祭神:{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}) *春日神社 (祭神:{{jalink|天児屋根命}}) *皇大神宮 (祭神:{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}) *祓戸社 (祭神:{{jalink|祓戸大神|Q11588504}}) === 例祭 === 10月28日であったが、現在は10月15日。 南菅田、北菅田、八条、長安寺、宮堂、額田部、西村など共催であり、当屋の{{jalink|渡御|Q11561919}}には、南菅田の菅田氏が一番に渡るのが例。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} *{{jalink|菅田神社|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市八条町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:菅田神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134888232}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 菅田神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 21 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 菅田比古命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月15日 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = 一夜松天神・八条天神社 |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''菅田神社'''（すがたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式 |access-date=2024-4-23}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。全国、{{jalink|鍛冶|Q838566}}の神の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}である。 古くは、現社地の南約300メートルにあり、{{jalink|大永|Q1193564}}3(1523)年ごろ、鎮座したと言われている{{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。 === 由緒 === 昔、美しい女神が{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂の「菅田池」から現れ、大和郡山市八条町の神楽田の所でしばらく休んでから、そこから西の方へ行って鎮座しました。すると、そこが一夜にして鬱蒼(うっそう)とした松林になったので、誰云うとなく、その土地を「一夜松」と呼び、今「一夜松天神」「八条天神社」とも呼ばれる現在の菅田神社が鎮座する社頭掲示板より。『佐保川其西方を流れ、菅田池畔にのぞむ、堀河百首平安時代後期の歌集で、長治2年(1105)ごろの成立か。に「をとめ子がすがたの池のはちす葉は心よげにも花さきにけり」と、以って景勝の地たるを知るべし』と、{{jalink|明治神社誌料}}に記されている。 === 概要 === 祭神は、菅田比古命。菅田比古命の祖で鍛冶の神とされる{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750}}の説{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=神祇資料 |access-date=2024-4-24}}もあるが、{{jalink|平群郡|Q11483501}}({{jalink|明治時代}}の八条村は平群郡及び、{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}の管轄で、昭和28年に{{jalink|郡山町 (奈良県)||郡山町}}に編入される)の{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}には、菅田比古命が記されている{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=平群郡 神社明細帳 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。菅田比古命は、菅田氏の祖で『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「菅田首{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}之後也」と記されている。 なお、近世以前は、八条天神神社・一夜松天神とも呼ばれ、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた。一夜にして松が生えたという奇跡が言い伝えられており、一夜松の幹の表皮部分が{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}に{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}されている{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。{{jalink|元和 (日本)|Q635245|元和}}5年(1619年)の{{jalink|石燈籠}}には、『一夜松金琳宮』と刻され、一夜松金琳寺という{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}があったことが推定される。所在は、{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂北菅田町の春日神社元は、「天久斯比止都命を祀ったものであろう」(天理市史より)の西方天理市史 上巻。 {{jalink|本殿|Q2044994}}は、{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}で{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}との間に{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}が設けられている。本殿前には、『天満宮御神前』と刻された石燈籠もあり、{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}の神社であったことがうかがえる。 また、同じく添下郡の式内社で大和郡山市筒井町に鎮座する、{{jalink|菅田比売神社|Q134893097}}(祭神は菅田比売命・天久斯麻比止都命2座特選神名牒及び、国立国会図書館サーチ「式内社の研究 第2巻」による。)ただし、添下郡神社明細帳では、伊豆能賣神1座。と、対をなしていると考えられている。 なお、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|小野市|Q911717}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (小野市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)と、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|近江八幡市|Q385461}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (近江八幡市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)の祭神は、天目一箇神である。 === 境内社 === *厳島神社 (祭神:{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}) *春日神社 (祭神:{{jalink|天児屋根命}}) *皇大神宮 (祭神:{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}) *祓戸社 (祭神:{{jalink|祓戸大神|Q11588504}}) === 例祭 === 10月28日であったが、現在は10月15日。 南菅田、北菅田、八条、長安寺、宮堂、額田部、西村など共催であり、当屋の{{jalink|渡御|Q11561919}}には、南菅田の菅田氏が一番に渡るのが例。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} *{{jalink|菅田神社|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式 |access-date=2024-4-23}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。全国、{{jalink|鍛冶|Q838566}}の神の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}である。 古くは、現社地の南約300メートルにあり、{{jalink|大永|Q1193564}}3(1523)年ごろ、鎮座したと言われている{{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。 === 由緒 === 昔、美しい女神が{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂の「菅田池」から現れ、大和郡山市八条町の神楽田の所でしばらく休んでから、そこから西の方へ行って鎮座しました。すると、そこが一夜にして鬱蒼(うっそう)とした松林になったので、誰云うとなく、その土地を「一夜松」と呼び、今「一夜松天神」「八条天神社」とも呼ばれる現在の菅田神社が鎮座する社頭掲示板より。『佐保川其西方を流れ、菅田池畔にのぞむ、堀河百首平安時代後期の歌集で、長治2年(1105)ごろの成立か。に「をとめ子がすがたの池のはちす葉は心よげにも花さきにけり」と、以って景勝の地たるを知るべし』と、{{jalink|明治神社誌料}}に記されている。 === 概要 === 祭神は、菅田比古命。菅田比古命の祖で鍛冶の神とされる{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750}}の説{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=神祇資料 |access-date=2024-4-24}}もあるが、{{jalink|平群郡|Q11483501}}({{jalink|明治時代}}の八条村は平群郡及び、{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}の管轄で、昭和28年に{{jalink|郡山町 (奈良県)||郡山町}}に編入される)の{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}には、菅田比古命が記されている{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=平群郡 神社明細帳 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。菅田比古命は、菅田氏の祖で『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「菅田首{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}之後也」と記されている。 なお、近世以前は、八条天神神社・一夜松天神とも呼ばれ、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた。一夜にして松が生えたという奇跡が言い伝えられており、一夜松の幹の表皮部分が{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}に{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}されている{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。{{jalink|元和 (日本)|Q635245|元和}}5年(1619年)の{{jalink|石燈籠}}には、『一夜松金琳宮』と刻され、一夜松金琳寺という{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}があったことが推定される。所在は、{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂北菅田町の春日神社元は、「天久斯比止都命を祀ったものであろう」(天理市史より)の西方天理市史 上巻。 {{jalink|本殿|Q2044994}}は、{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}で{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}との間に{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}が設けられている。本殿前には、『天満宮御神前』と刻された石燈籠もあり、{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}の神社であったことがうかがえる。 また、同じく添下郡の式内社で大和郡山市筒井町に鎮座する、{{jalink|菅田比売神社|Q134893097}}(祭神は菅田比売命・天久斯麻比止都命2座特選神名牒及び、国立国会図書館サーチ「式内社の研究 第2巻」による。)ただし、添下郡神社明細帳では、伊豆能賣神1座。と、対をなしていると考えられている。 なお、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|小野市|Q911717}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (小野市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)と、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|近江八幡市|Q385461}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (近江八幡市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)の祭神は、天目一箇神である。 === 境内社 === *厳島神社 (祭神:{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}) *春日神社 (祭神:{{jalink|天児屋根命}}) *皇大神宮 (祭神:{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}) *祓戸社 (祭神:{{jalink|祓戸大神|Q11588504}}) === 例祭 === 10月28日であったが、現在は10月15日。 南菅田、北菅田、八条、長安寺、宮堂、額田部、西村など共催であり、当屋の{{jalink|渡御|Q11561919}}には、南菅田の菅田氏が一番に渡るのが例。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} *{{jalink|菅田神社|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:need translation]]

(P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市八条町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:菅田神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134888232}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 菅田神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 21 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 菅田比古命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月15日 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = 一夜松天神・八条天神社 |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''菅田神社'''（すがたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式 |access-date=2024-4-23}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。全国、{{jalink|鍛冶|Q838566}}の神の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}である。 古くは、現社地の南約300メートルにあり、{{jalink|大永|Q1193564}}3(1523)年ごろ、鎮座したと言われている{{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。 === 由緒 === 昔、美しい女神が{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂の「菅田池」から現れ、大和郡山市八条町の神楽田の所でしばらく休んでから、そこから西の方へ行って鎮座しました。すると、そこが一夜にして鬱蒼(うっそう)とした松林になったので、誰云うとなく、その土地を「一夜松」と呼び、今「一夜松天神」「八条天神社」とも呼ばれる現在の菅田神社が鎮座する社頭掲示板より。『佐保川其西方を流れ、菅田池畔にのぞむ、堀河百首平安時代後期の歌集で、長治2年(1105)ごろの成立か。に「をとめ子がすがたの池のはちす葉は心よげにも花さきにけり」と、以って景勝の地たるを知るべし』と、{{jalink|明治神社誌料}}に記されている。 === 概要 === 祭神は、菅田比古命。菅田比古命の祖で鍛冶の神とされる{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750}}の説{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=神祇資料 |access-date=2024-4-24}}もあるが、{{jalink|平群郡|Q11483501}}({{jalink|明治時代}}の八条村は平群郡及び、{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}の管轄で、昭和28年に{{jalink|郡山町 (奈良県)||郡山町}}に編入される)の{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}には、菅田比古命が記されている{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=平群郡 神社明細帳 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。菅田比古命は、菅田氏の祖で『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「菅田首{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}之後也」と記されている。 なお、近世以前は、八条天神神社・一夜松天神とも呼ばれ、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた。一夜にして松が生えたという奇跡が言い伝えられており、一夜松の幹の表皮部分が{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}に{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}されている{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。{{jalink|元和 (日本)|Q635245|元和}}5年(1619年)の{{jalink|石燈籠}}には、『一夜松金琳宮』と刻され、一夜松金琳寺という{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}があったことが推定される。所在は、{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂北菅田町の春日神社元は、「天久斯比止都命を祀ったものであろう」(天理市史より)の西方天理市史 上巻。 {{jalink|本殿|Q2044994}}は、{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}で{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}との間に{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}が設けられている。本殿前には、『天満宮御神前』と刻された石燈籠もあり、{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}の神社であったことがうかがえる。 また、同じく添下郡の式内社で大和郡山市筒井町に鎮座する、{{jalink|菅田比売神社|Q134893097}}(祭神は菅田比売命・天久斯麻比止都命2座特選神名牒及び、国立国会図書館サーチ「式内社の研究 第2巻」による。)ただし、添下郡神社明細帳では、伊豆能賣神1座。と、対をなしていると考えられている。 なお、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|小野市|Q911717}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (小野市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)と、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|近江八幡市|Q385461}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (近江八幡市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)の祭神は、天目一箇神である。 === 境内社 === *厳島神社 (祭神:{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}) *春日神社 (祭神:{{jalink|天児屋根命}}) *皇大神宮 (祭神:{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}) *祓戸社 (祭神:{{jalink|祓戸大神|Q11588504}}) === 例祭 === 10月28日であったが、現在は10月15日。 南菅田、北菅田、八条、長安寺、宮堂、額田部、西村など共催であり、当屋の{{jalink|渡御|Q11561919}}には、南菅田の菅田氏が一番に渡るのが例。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} *{{jalink|菅田神社|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市八条町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:菅田神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134888232}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 菅田神社 | 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=大和郡山市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町619 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 21 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |祭神 = 菅田比古命 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |例祭 = 10月15日 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = 一夜松天神・八条天神社 |札所等 = |神事 = |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Japan Nara }} '''菅田神社'''（すがたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|大和郡山市|Q118213}}八条町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|添下郡|Q11560415}}の{{jalink|式内社}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式 |access-date=2024-4-23}}で、旧社格は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。全国、{{jalink|鍛冶|Q838566}}の神の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}である。 古くは、現社地の南約300メートルにあり、{{jalink|大永|Q1193564}}3(1523)年ごろ、鎮座したと言われている{{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。 === 由緒 === 昔、美しい女神が{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂の「菅田池」から現れ、大和郡山市八条町の神楽田の所でしばらく休んでから、そこから西の方へ行って鎮座しました。すると、そこが一夜にして鬱蒼(うっそう)とした松林になったので、誰云うとなく、その土地を「一夜松」と呼び、今「一夜松天神」「八条天神社」とも呼ばれる現在の菅田神社が鎮座する社頭掲示板より。『佐保川其西方を流れ、菅田池畔にのぞむ、堀河百首平安時代後期の歌集で、長治2年(1105)ごろの成立か。に「をとめ子がすがたの池のはちす葉は心よげにも花さきにけり」と、以って景勝の地たるを知るべし』と、{{jalink|明治神社誌料}}に記されている。 === 概要 === 祭神は、菅田比古命。菅田比古命の祖で鍛冶の神とされる{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750}}の説{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=神祇資料 |access-date=2024-4-24}}もあるが、{{jalink|平群郡|Q11483501}}({{jalink|明治時代}}の八条村は平群郡及び、{{jalink|生駒郡|Q971003}}の管轄で、昭和28年に{{jalink|郡山町 (奈良県)||郡山町}}に編入される)の{{jalink|神社明細帳|Q11590704}}には、菅田比古命が記されている{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=平群郡 神社明細帳 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。菅田比古命は、菅田氏の祖で『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「菅田首{{jalink|天目一箇神|Q10016750|天久斯麻比止都命}}之後也」と記されている。 なお、近世以前は、八条天神神社・一夜松天神とも呼ばれ、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた。一夜にして松が生えたという奇跡が言い伝えられており、一夜松の幹の表皮部分が{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}に{{jalink|奉納|Q2601788}}されている{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=菅田神社 |access-date=2024-4-23}}。{{jalink|元和 (日本)|Q635245|元和}}5年(1619年)の{{jalink|石燈籠}}には、『一夜松金琳宮』と刻され、一夜松金琳寺という{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}があったことが推定される。所在は、{{jalink|天理市|Q502196}}二階堂北菅田町の春日神社元は、「天久斯比止都命を祀ったものであろう」(天理市史より)の西方天理市史 上巻。 {{jalink|本殿|Q2044994}}は、{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}で{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}との間に{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}が設けられている。本殿前には、『天満宮御神前』と刻された石燈籠もあり、{{jalink|天神信仰|Q66890725}}の神社であったことがうかがえる。 また、同じく添下郡の式内社で大和郡山市筒井町に鎮座する、{{jalink|菅田比売神社|Q134893097}}(祭神は菅田比売命・天久斯麻比止都命2座特選神名牒及び、国立国会図書館サーチ「式内社の研究 第2巻」による。)ただし、添下郡神社明細帳では、伊豆能賣神1座。と、対をなしていると考えられている。 なお、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|小野市|Q911717}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (小野市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)と、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|近江八幡市|Q385461}}の{{jalink|菅田神社 (近江八幡市)||菅田神社}}(式内社)の祭神は、天目一箇神である。 === 境内社 === *厳島神社 (祭神:{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}) *春日神社 (祭神:{{jalink|天児屋根命}}) *皇大神宮 (祭神:{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}) *祓戸社 (祭神:{{jalink|祓戸大神|Q11588504}}) === 例祭 === 10月28日であったが、現在は10月15日。 南菅田、北菅田、八条、長安寺、宮堂、額田部、西村など共催であり、当屋の{{jalink|渡御|Q11561919}}には、南菅田の菅田氏が一番に渡るのが例。 === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} *{{jalink|菅田神社|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:Need translation]] (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市八条町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:菅田神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134888232}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugata Shrine | Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Yamatokōriyama City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |Address = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{jalink|blacksmithing|Q838566}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{jalink|Daiei|Q1193564}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{jalink|Meiji Jinja Shiryō}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} of {{jalink|Heguri District|Q11483501}} (Hachijō Village in the {{jalink|Meiji period}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, and was incorporated into {{jalink|Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)||Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} in the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{jalink|Genna (Japan)|Q635245|Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{jalink|main hall|Q2044994}} is built in the {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} style, with a {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} installed between it and the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{jalink|Tenjin worship|Q66890725}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{jalink|Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ono City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ono City|Q911717}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}, and {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ōmihachiman City|Q385461}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{jalink|procession|Q11561919}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Sugata Shrine|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |Address = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{jalink|blacksmithing|Q838566}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{jalink|Daiei|Q1193564}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{jalink|Meiji Jinja Shiryō}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} of {{jalink|Heguri District|Q11483501}} (Hachijō Village in the {{jalink|Meiji period}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, and was incorporated into {{jalink|Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)||Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} in the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{jalink|Genna (Japan)|Q635245|Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{jalink|main hall|Q2044994}} is built in the {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} style, with a {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} installed between it and the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{jalink|Tenjin worship|Q66890725}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{jalink|Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ono City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ono City|Q911717}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}, and {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ōmihachiman City|Q385461}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{jalink|procession|Q11561919}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Sugata Shrine|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}

|Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{jalink|blacksmithing|Q838566}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{jalink|Daiei|Q1193564}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{jalink|Meiji Jinja Shiryō}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} of {{jalink|Heguri District|Q11483501}} (Hachijō Village in the {{jalink|Meiji period}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, and was incorporated into {{jalink|Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)||Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} in the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{jalink|Genna (Japan)|Q635245|Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{jalink|main hall|Q2044994}} is built in the {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} style, with a {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} installed between it and the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{jalink|Tenjin worship|Q66890725}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{jalink|Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ono City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ono City|Q911717}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}, and {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ōmihachiman City|Q385461}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{jalink|procession|Q11561919}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Sugata Shrine|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}

{'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市八条町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:菅田神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134888232}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugata Shrine | Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Yamatokōriyama City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |Address = {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{jalink|Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Soekami District|Q11560415}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{jalink|sōsha|Q1107129}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{jalink|blacksmithing|Q838566}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{jalink|Daiei|Q1193564}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{jalink|Meiji Jinja Shiryō}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{jalink|Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704}} of {{jalink|Heguri District|Q11483501}} (Hachijō Village in the {{jalink|Meiji period}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{jalink|Ikoma District|Q971003}}, and was incorporated into {{jalink|Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)||Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{jalink|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{jalink|dedicated|Q2601788}} in the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{jalink|Genna (Japan)|Q635245|Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{jalink|jingū-ji|Q1074971}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{jalink|Tenri City|Q502196}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{jalink|main hall|Q2044994}} is built in the {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} style, with a {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} installed between it and the {{jalink|worship hall|Q3125745}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{jalink|Tenjin worship|Q66890725}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{jalink|Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ono City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ono City|Q911717}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}, and {{jalink|Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)||Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{jalink|Ōmihachiman City|Q385461}}, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{jalink|Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{jalink|Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{jalink|procession|Q11561919}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} * {{jalink|Sugata Shrine|Q128845721}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}

Sugata Shrine | Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Yamatokōriyama City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{ill|lt=blacksmithing|Q838566|WD=Q838566|1=Metalsmith}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugata Shrine (Q134888232)}}[[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{ill|lt=blacksmithing|Q838566|WD=Q838566|1=Metalsmith}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県大和郡山市八条町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:菅田神社 (大和郡山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134888232}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Sugata Shrine | Image = {{Image wanted|cat=Yamatokōriyama City}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{ill|lt=blacksmithing|Q838566|WD=Q838566|1=Metalsmith}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社|すかたしんしや]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]
