Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{ill|lt=blacksmithing|Q838566|WD=Q838566|1=Metalsmith}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|36|21|N|135|47|22|E}}}} |Address = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{ill|lt=blacksmithing|Q838566|WD=Q838566|1=Metalsmith}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]]

Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}} 619 Hachijō-chō |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 36 |Latitude seconds = 21 |Longitude degrees = 135 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 22 |ISO = JP-29 |Enshrined deity = Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Annual festival = October 15 |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Other names = Ichiya-matsu Tenjin / Hachijō Tenjin Shrine |Pilgrimage designation = |Rituals = |Label position = top |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Sugata Shrine''' (すがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hachijō-chō, {{ill|lt=Yamatokōriyama City|Q118213|WD=Q118213|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Soekami District|Q11560415|WD=Q11560415|1=Soejimo district}}{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki |access-date=2024-4-23}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is regarded nationwide as the {{ill|lt=sōsha|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}} (principal shrine) of the god of {{ill|lt=blacksmithing|Q838566|WD=Q838566|1=Metalsmith}}. In ancient times, the shrine was located about 300 meters south of its present site, and it is said to have been transferred to its current location around the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Daiei|Q1193564|WD=Q1193564|1=Daiei (era)}} (1523){{Cite web |url=https://jun-yu-roku.com/yamato-heguri-hachijo-sugata/ |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. === History === Long ago, a beautiful goddess appeared from “Sugata Pond” in Nikaidō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}. She rested for a while in Kagurada in Hachijō-chō, Yamatokōriyama City, and then went westward and took up residence there. Overnight, the place became a dense pine grove, and people began to call the area “Ichiya-matsu” (One-Night Pine). Thus, the present Sugata Shrine—also known as “Ichiya-matsu Tenjin” or “Hachijō Tenjin Shrine”—came to be enshrined thereFrom the shrine notice board. The {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji Jinja Shiryō|1=UNKNOWN}} records: “The Saho River flows to the west, overlooking the banks of Sugata Pond. In the *Horigawa Hyakushu*A poetry anthology from the late Heian period, likely compiled around the 2nd year of Chōji (1105)., it is written: ‘The lotus leaves on Sugata Pond, where the maiden dwells, bloom with a gentle heart.’ From this one may know that it was a place of scenic beauty.” === Overview === The enshrined deity is Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto. There is also a theory that the deity is {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}, an ancestor of Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto and regarded as a god of blacksmithing{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/11 |title=Shingi Shiryō |access-date=2024-4-24}}. However, the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}} of {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|WD=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} (Hachijō Village in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}} was under the jurisdiction of Heguri District and {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|WD=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, and was incorporated into {{ill|lt=Kōriyama Town (Nara Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōriyama Town}} in 1953) records Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto as the enshrined deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Heguri District Shrine Registry |access-date=2024-4-23}}. Sugata-hiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Sugata clan, and the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} states: “The Sugata no Obito are descendants of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|WD=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}}.” Before the early modern period, the shrine was also called Hachijō Tenjin Shrine or Ichiya-matsu Tenjin, and it enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. A legend tells of a miracle in which a pine tree grew overnight, and part of the bark of the Ichiya Pine is {{ill|lt=dedicated|Q2601788|WD=Q2601788|1=Votive offering}} in the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}{{Cite web |url=https://yamatotk.web.fc2.com/kouriyama/sugata.htm |title=Sugata Shrine |access-date=2024-4-23}}. A stone lantern from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} (1619) bears the inscription “Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-gū,” suggesting the existence of a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} called Ichiya-matsu Kinrin-ji. Its location is estimated to have been west of Kasuga Shrine in Nikaidō Kita-Sugata-chō, {{ill|lt=Tenri City|Q502196|WD=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}Originally, “it was probably dedicated to Ame-no-Kushi-hitotsu-no-Mikoto” (from *Tenri City History*).Tenri City History, Volume 1. The {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is built in the {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, with a {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699|1=Heiden (Shinto)}} installed between it and the {{ill|lt=worship hall|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}. In front of the main hall stands a stone lantern inscribed “Before the Deity of Tenmangū,” indicating that the shrine was associated with {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}}. It is also thought to form a pair with {{ill|lt=Sugata-hime Shrine|Q134893097|WD=Q134893097|1=Sugatahime Shrine}}, another Shikinaisha of Soekami District located in Tsutsui-chō, Yamatokōriyama City (enshrining Sugata-hime-no-Mikoto and Ame-no-Kushi-mahito-tsu-no-Mikoto, two deitiesAccording to *Tokusen Shinmeichō* and the National Diet Library Search “Research on Shikinaisha, Vol. 2.”)However, the Soekami District Shrine Registry lists only one deity: Izunome-no-Kami.. The enshrined deities of {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ono City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ono City|Q911717|WD=Q911717|1=Ono, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, and {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine (Ōmihachiman City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugata Shrine}} (a Shikinaisha) in {{ill|lt=Ōmihachiman City|Q385461|WD=Q385461|1=Ōmihachiman}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, are Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami. === Auxiliary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}) * Kasuga Shrine (Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}) * Kōtaijingū (Deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) * Haraedo Shrine (Deity: {{ill|lt=Haraedo Ōkami|Q11588504|WD=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}) === Annual festival === It was formerly held on October 28, but is now held on October 15. It is jointly celebrated by Minami-Sugata, Kita-Sugata, Hachijō, Chōan-ji, Miyadō, Nukatabe, Nishimura, and other areas. During the {{ill|lt=procession|Q11561919|WD=Q11561919|1=procession}} of the hosting household, it is customary for the Sugata clan of Minami-Sugata to proceed first. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group=note}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=Sugata Shrine|Q128845721|WD=Q128845721|1=Sugata Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugata Shrine (Yamatokōriyama)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamatokoriyama, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
